general_disclaimer: This app provides general wellness information for Indian-market longevity optimization. It is NOT medical advice. All recommendations must be evaluated against individual medical history, current medications, pregnancy/lactation status, and lab values. Users with diagnosed conditions (cardiovascular disease, diabetes, autoimmune disease, thyroid dysfunction, pregnancy) MUST consult a qualified physician before initiating any supplement, dietary, or thermal/exercise protocol. Many products referenced (NMN, peptides, gene therapy, stem cells, red-light devices) are not approved for consumer use under FSSAI / CDSCO / ICMR-DBT Indian regulations; those are described for context only and are not recommended as consumer pathways. Therapeutic disease-prevention claims under the Drugs & Magic Remedies (Objectionable Advertisements) Act 1954 and the FSSAI Health Claims Regulation 2022 require substantiation; we use general-wellness framing only. Prices, product availability, and regulatory status may change — re-verify before purchase.

contraindications.subject: KSM-66 ashwagandha (Withania somnifera)
contraindicated_in: autoimmune disease (Hashimoto's thyroiditis, Graves, lupus/SLE, rheumatoid arthritis, multiple sclerosis)
concurrent sedatives / benzodiazepines / barbiturates / alcohol (additive CNS depression)
concurrent thyroid hormone replacement (may potentiate T4/T3)
concurrent immunosuppressants (tacrolimus, cyclosporine, mycophenolate, post-transplant) — may antagonize immunosuppression
scheduled surgery within 2 weeks (sedative / anaesthetic interaction)
significant hepatic impairment (rare DILI case reports 2019-2023)
contraindications.reason: Ashwagandha is a documented immunostimulant (↑Th1, ↑NK activity) and can worsen autoimmune flares. It raises free T4/T3 in euthyroid and hypothyroid subjects (Sharma 2018, PMID 29076790), which is desirable in hypothyroidism but risky in hyperthyroidism or on replacement therapy. CNS-depressant potentiation is documented in animal models and traditional use. Emerging case reports of ashwagandha-associated hepatitis (LiverTox NIH, 2023).
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: PMID 29076790 (Sharma 2018, thyroid effect); LiverTox NIH (2023) ashwagandha hepatotoxicity review; ICMR-NIN AYUSH safety monograph
contraindications.severity: absolute (pregnancy, hyperthyroidism, active autoimmune flare); relative (sedatives, surgery, thyroid replacement)

contraindications.subject: Mustard oil (cold-press, high-erucic)
contraindicated_in: established coronary artery disease (CAD) / prior MI
elevated ApoB or LDL-C on lipid-lowering therapy
paediatric populations (infants/children) as sole cooking oil
pregnancy at high daily intake (>1-2 tbsp/day)
contraindications.reason: Mustard oil contains 30-54% erucic acid. EFSA 2016 set a Tolerable Daily Intake of 7 mg/kg bw/day for erucic acid — 1-2 tbsp/day can exceed this for a 60 kg adult. Long-term high-erucic diets cause myocardial lipidosis in animal models; human cardioprotective evidence is weaker but ICMR-NIN 2024 Dietary Guidelines recommend oil rotation specifically because of mustard-oil erucic-acid concerns. Mustard oil has ZERO of the oleocanthal/hydroxytyrosol polyphenols that drive EVOO's EU-approved LDL-oxidation-reduction claim — it is not a polyphenol-functional substitute.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: EFSA Journal 2016;14(11):4593 (erucic acid scientific opinion); ICMR-NIN Dietary Guidelines for Indians 2024 (edible oils section); FSSAI Regulation on Fats & Oils; EU Commission Regulation 432/2012 (olive polyphenol health claim)
contraindications.severity: relative (general adult); absolute-relative (established CAD, paediatric as sole oil)

contraindications.subject: A2 ghee / clarified butter (daily >1 tsp)
contraindicated_in: on statin therapy with unmet LDL targets
contraindications.reason: Ghee is ~62% saturated fat. Saturated-fat load is identical between A1 and A2 dairy; the A1/A2 casein distinction is about gut tolerance, not atherogenicity. Indian populations (LASI, ICMR-INDIAB) have elevated background ApoB and high-prevalence Lp(a). Daily ghee in such a population without a lipid-panel precondition is inconsistent with the app's Mediterranean/plant-forward framing.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: ICMR-NIN Dietary Guidelines for Indians 2024; AHA 2021 Dietary Guidance scientific statement on saturated fat; LASI wave-1 lipid epidemiology
contraindications.severity: relative (gate behind ApoB/LDL measurement; cap at flavor-only <1 tsp/day if lipids uncontrolled)

contraindications.subject: Infrared / Finnish-style sauna, heat therapy
contraindicated_in: unstable angina / recent MI within 3 months
pregnancy (especially first trimester — neural-tube and hyperthermia risk)
severe hypotension or autonomic dysfunction (risk of syncope)
implanted medical devices not rated for heat (some pumps/pacemakers check manufacturer)
contraindications.reason: Sauna induces significant peripheral vasodilation, tachycardia (HR to 100-150), and plasma-volume shift. The Laukkanen Finnish cohort was 2,315 male Finnish subjects with lifelong sauna adaptation using traditional Finnish dry-humid saunas — generalizability to Indian women, infrared saunas, and users in a hot ambient climate (Indian summer heat stress already high) is unvalidated. Additional risk in Indian climate: dehydration and electrolyte loss are pre-existing. Pregnancy hyperthermia risk: core temp >39°C in first trimester is associated with NTD.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: Laukkanen JAMA IM 2015 (PMID 25705824); 2018 BMC Med Laukkanen mixed-sex data; ACC/AHA heat-therapy consensus; ACOG pregnancy hyperthermia guidance
contraindications.severity: absolute (uncontrolled HT, recent MI, aortic stenosis, first-trimester pregnancy); relative (elderly frailty, hot-climate hydration)

contraindications.subject: Continuous Glucose Monitor (CGM) in non-diabetic adults
contraindicated_in: history of disordered eating / anorexia / orthorexia
significant health anxiety or OCD tendencies
users without access to physician interpretation
contraindications.reason: CGM for non-diabetics is NOT endorsed by ICMR, Endocrine Society, or ADA 2024 Standards of Care. Postprandial glucose excursions to 140-180 mg/dL in healthy non-diabetics are physiological, not pathological. Free-floating CGM data without clinical interpretation creates false-positive prediabetes anxiety and can trigger or worsen restrictive eating patterns. Abbott FreeStyle Libre sensor accuracy is adequate; the issue is interpretation, not measurement.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: ADA Standards of Medical Care in Diabetes 2024; Endocrine Society position 2022 on CGM in non-diabetics; ICMR Indian Diabetes Guidelines
contraindications.severity: relative (requires interpretation layer; not recommended as free-standing consumer tool)

contraindications.subject: NMN (nicotinamide mononucleotide)
contraindicated_in: all Indian consumer use — no FSSAI nutraceutical listing
active malignancy (theoretical concern: NAD+ restoration may support proliferation of NAD-dependent tumours)
contraindications.reason: NMN has no approved nutraceutical status under FSSAI Nutraceutical Regulations 2022 (Schedule VII). US FDA removed NMN from NDI status in October 2022. Indian NMN products are grey-market imports. Oncology theoretical concern based on Warburg-tumour NAD dependence (preclinical). No human safety data in pregnancy.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: US FDA NDI rejection letter on NMN (October 2022); FSSAI Nutraceutical Regulations 2022 Schedule VII; no ICMR approval
contraindications.severity: absolute (regulatory — do not recommend purchase in India); relative (oncology, pregnancy)

contraindications.subject: Moringa (Moringa oleifera) leaves and powder
contraindicated_in: pregnancy (especially bark/root/seed extracts — uterotonic activity in traditional literature)
concurrent levothyroxine (moringa has thyroid-active compounds; timing separation needed)
commercial powdered products without heavy-metal COA (Pb/Cd/As risk)
contraindications.reason: Moringa is a phytoremediator — it preferentially accumulates heavy metals (Pb, Cd, As) from contaminated soil, a documented issue in Indian commercial supply. FSSAI plant-food heavy-metal standards apply. Bark/root extracts have uterotonic activity in ethnobotanical and rodent literature; leaves are generally considered safer but still not recommended in pregnancy without supervision. Dried powder loses myrosinase enzyme, so glucomoringin → moringin conversion in gut requires co-ingestion of a myrosinase source (e.g., mustard seed) or fresh leaves.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: Waterman 2014 (moringa isothiocyanate processing); FSSAI heavy-metal standards for plant foods; Indian ethnobotanical safety review on moringa uterotonic activity
contraindications.severity: absolute (pregnancy, bark/root/seed); relative (leaves — require COA-verified brand)

contraindications.subject: Amla (Phyllanthus emblica) high-dose
contraindicated_in: concurrent anticoagulants (warfarin, DOACs — additive bleeding risk from vitamin C + tannin effects)
severe GERD or peptic ulcer (acidic load)
oxalate kidney stone history at very high intake (vitamin C → oxalate conversion)
contraindications.reason: High-dose vitamin C (via amla) can potentiate anticoagulant-associated bleeding and at very high chronic doses converts to oxalate. Acidic fruit load can aggravate GERD. Note bioavailability: oral vitamin C absorption plateaus at ~200 mg/day (Levine et al.), so super-physiological amla intake does not proportionately raise plasma ascorbate.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: Levine PNAS 1996 on oral vitamin C pharmacokinetics (PMID 8623000); NIH ODS vitamin C fact sheet

contraindications.subject: Curcumin / turmeric high-dose (with piperine)
contraindicated_in: gallbladder disease / biliary obstruction (curcumin is choleretic)
concurrent chemotherapy (CYP3A4 interactions with docetaxel, cyclophosphamide, others)
known susceptibility to DILI (case reports of curcumin-associated hepatitis 2022-2023)
contraindications.reason: Curcumin requires piperine or lipid matrix for absorption (bioavailability <1% without). Piperine inhibits CYP3A4 and P-gp — significant drug-interaction potential. Choleretic effect contraindicates in gallstones. Iron chelation documented. Emerging DILI case reports with high-dose supplements (especially piperine-enhanced formulations).
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: Shoba 1998 piperine-curcumin bioavailability (PMID 9619120); LiverTox NIH curcumin entry 2023; NCCIH turmeric safety

contraindications.subject: Pomegranate (Punica granatum) and ellagitannins
contraindicated_in: concurrent statins (CYP3A4 inhibition at high juice intake — like grapefruit juice, though milder)
contraindications.reason: Pomegranate juice inhibits CYP3A4 at high intake, comparable to grapefruit (though clinical significance contested). BP-lowering effect can be additive to antihypertensives. Urolithin-A conversion requires specific gut microbiota — only ~40% of humans are producers; non-producers get cardiometabolic benefit but not mitophagy benefit.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: Hidaka 2005 pomegranate CYP3A4 (PMID 15680228); Tomas-Barberan 2014 on urolithin-A metabotypes

contraindications.subject: Peptides / experimental therapies (BPC-157, TB-500, epithalon, cerebrolysin, follistatin gene therapy, mesenchymal stem cells)
contraindications.reason: Peptides (BPC-157, TB-500, epithalon) are not approved by CDSCO for consumer use. Stem-cell therapies for longevity indications are restricted under ICMR-DBT National Guidelines 2017 and CDSCO — only approved for specific clinical indications. Gene therapy requires DCGI approval. Describing these as replicable consumer protocol items is a regulatory-advice failure.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: ICMR-DBT National Guidelines for Stem Cell Research 2017; CDSCO Medical Devices Rules 2017; DCGI gene therapy framework

contraindications.subject: Red-light therapy caps / devices with medical claims
contraindicated_in: active scalp malignancy or suspicious lesions
uncharacterized pigmented lesions in treatment field
contraindications.reason: Medical-claim RLT devices fall under CDSCO Medical Devices Rules 2017. Only CDSCO-registered devices should be recommended. Photosensitizing drug interactions documented. Lifestyle/cosmetic framing required under Drugs & Magic Remedies Act.

contraindications.subject: Hyperbaric oxygen therapy (HBOT)
contraindicated_in: middle-ear / sinus disease with inability to equalize
contraindications.reason: Pneumothorax is an absolute contraindication per UHMS (Undersea & Hyperbaric Medical Society). Oxygen toxicity seizures and middle-ear barotrauma are recognized adverse events. Longevity HBOT at 1.3-2.0 ATA is off-label in India — only specific CDSCO-approved indications exist.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: UHMS Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy Indications (15th ed.); CDSCO approved indications list

contraindications.subject: Intensive blood pressure control (SPRINT-style SBP <120)
contraindicated_in: diabetes mellitus (EXCLUDED from SPRINT — generalizability fails)
age >80 with frailty (high orthostatic / fall / AKI risk)
contraindications.reason: SPRINT 2015 explicitly excluded these populations. Intensive BP control has documented harms (syncope, AKI, hyponatremia, electrolyte disturbance, fall/hip-fracture in elderly). J-curve: below DBP 60 in CAD patients, event risk may rise. Indian hypertensive population has high diabetes comorbidity — SPRINT generalizability is limited.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: SPRINT NEJM 2015 (PMID 26551272); SPRINT subgroup analyses; Messerli J-curve meta-analyses

contraindications.subject: Time-restricted eating (6-hour pre-noon window) / prolonged fasting
contraindicated_in: type 2 diabetes on insulin or sulfonylureas (hypoglycemia risk)
underweight (BMI <18.5) — in Indian population note thin-fat phenotype
concurrent medications requiring food (metformin XR tolerability, NSAIDs)
contraindications.reason: Hypoglycemia risk on sulfonylureas/insulin; amenorrhea and bone-loss risk in low-BMI women; paediatric growth concerns. No Indian-population TRE mortality data at the 6-hour pre-noon window. Indian meal timing intersects with family/cultural eating patterns — adherence and relational cost should be considered.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: ADA 2024 Standards of Care (hypoglycemia prevention on sulfonylureas); NEDA clinical guidance on fasting in ED history
contraindications.severity: absolute (T1DM without supervision, pregnancy, ED history); relative (others)

contraindications.subject: Extended sleep duration >8.5 hours
contraindicated_in: users with untreated sleep-disordered breathing (OSA) — extended time in bed without treatment worsens outcomes
depressive disorders (hypersomnia may be symptom, not target)
chronic fatigue where extended sleep is symptomatic rather than protective
contraindications.reason: Multiple mortality meta-analyses show a U-shaped curve: sleep >9 hours is associated with HIGHER all-cause mortality (though reverse causation — occult illness — confounds). The 8.5-hour target is Bryan-specific self-report, not a population-level optimum. For users with undiagnosed OSA, prolonged time in bed without CPAP is not protective.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: Cappuccio Sleep 2010 meta-analysis U-shape (PMID 20469800); AASM clinical practice guideline on sleep duration 2015

contraindications.subject: ALA (walnut, flax, chia) as sole omega-3 source
contraindicated_in: established cardiovascular disease requiring EPA/DHA cardioprotection
pregnancy (DHA requirement not met by ALA conversion)
South Asian users with likely FADS1/FADS2 minor-allele burden
contraindications.reason: ALA→EPA conversion in humans is 5-10%; ALA→DHA conversion <0.5%. FADS1/FADS2 polymorphisms common in South Asians further reduce conversion (Chilton/Sergeant). High linoleic-acid (refined Indian oil) background suppresses conversion further. ALA walnut intake is NOT equivalent to marine EPA/DHA for cardioprotection.
contraindications.source_pmid_or_guideline: Brenna 2009 on ALA conversion efficiency (PMID 19269799); Chilton 2014 on FADS polymorphism in South Asians (PMID 24566443)
contraindications.severity: relative (inform users; retain algal/fish oil line item)

indian_genetic_context.relevance: ALA → EPA / DHA conversion inefficiency — the minor/ancestral allele associated with reduced Δ5/Δ6 desaturase activity is high-frequency in South Asian populations (Chilton et al. 2014). Compounds the already-low baseline ALA conversion (5-10% to EPA, <0.5% to DHA). Users relying on walnut/flax/chia as sole omega-3 source may achieve sub-therapeutic tissue EPA/DHA.

Ergothioneine cellular uptake and tissue retention — OCTN1 is the primary ergothioneine transporter. Loss-of-function variants result in near-zero plasma ergothioneine retention regardless of dietary intake. Polymorphism frequency variable; Indian-population allele frequencies are incompletely characterized but should be assumed variable. Mushroom ergothioneine-mediated benefits (MCI protection in Singapore cohort) may not transfer to OCTN1-low-function users.
indian_genetic_context.source_pmid_or_guideline: Pfeiffer et al. on OCTN1 ergothioneine kinetics (source: null, needs_verification: true); Beelman dietary ergothioneine targets

indian_genetic_context.relevance: Apolipoprotein E ε4 carriers have elevated LDL-cholesterol responsiveness to saturated fat, higher late-onset Alzheimer risk, and differential response to DHA supplementation (some data suggest ε4 carriers may paradoxically need higher DHA due to impaired BBB transport). Indian APOE ε4 prevalence ~7-10% (regional variation). Relevant to ghee/saturated-fat dose ceilings and to omega-3 strategy in older users.
indian_genetic_context.source_pmid_or_guideline: source: null, needs_verification: true (Indian APOE prevalence — regional studies exist but not consolidated here)

indian_genetic_context.variant: LPA (LPA gene — isoform size / Kringle IV repeats) — elevated Lp(a)
indian_genetic_context.relevance: Lipoprotein(a) is a genetically determined, independent, causal CV risk factor. South Asian populations have elevated prevalence of high Lp(a) (>50 mg/dL or >125 nmol/L) — estimates 25-30% vs ~20% Western. Lp(a) does not respond meaningfully to diet or statins; it is identification-only (currently). Highly relevant to any lab-panel recommendation — ApoB alone underestimates risk in South Asians without Lp(a) measurement.
indian_genetic_context.source_pmid_or_guideline: Enas INTERHEART Lp(a) South Asian data; Reyes-Soffer 2022 AHA scientific statement on Lp(a)

indian_genetic_context.relevance: Reduced methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase activity affects folate metabolism and homocysteine. Indian-population minor-allele frequency estimated ~10-20% TT homozygotes regionally. May inform B-vitamin supplementation strategy (methylfolate vs folic acid).

High prevalence in certain Indian subpopulations (~8-10% in some communities). Relevant because high-dose vitamin C (amla megadose), fava beans, and certain drugs trigger hemolysis. Relevant to amla supplementation at super-physiological doses.
indian_genetic_context.source_pmid_or_guideline: ICMR-NIN monographs on G6PD; NIH G6PD MedlinePlus

dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.ceiling: ~200 mg/day absorption plateau; plasma saturation at ~400 mg/day; intakes above this result in dose-proportional renal excretion
dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.source: Levine et al. PNAS 1996 (PMID 8623000); NIH ODS Vitamin C Fact Sheet

dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.ceiling: <1% systemic bioavailability as parent compound; circulating metabolites (protocatechuic acid, phenolic acid derivatives) mediate most effects; bioavailability heavily dependent on gut microbiome and food matrix
dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.source: Manach 2005 polyphenol bioavailability review (PMID 15640486); Kay 2017 anthocyanin metabolism

dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.ceiling: 5-10% in young women; 2-5% in men; further reduced (likely 1-3%) in FADS1/FADS2 minor-allele carriers common in South Asians
<0.5% in most adults; effectively negligible in men and older adults
<1% oral bioavailability without piperine / lipid / nanoformulation; 2000% increase with 20 mg piperine per 2g curcumin

No hard ceiling but target plasma maintenance requires ~3-5 mg/day dietary intake; 50g fresh oyster mushroom (~90% water) delivers only ~0.5-3 mg (dossier figures are dry-weight); OCTN1 genotype caps tissue retention regardless of intake

Only ~40% of humans are 'producer' metabotype; non-producers convert little to no urolithin-A from pomegranate/walnut ellagitannins. Producer status is partially inducible with sustained intake (4-8 weeks).
dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.source: Tomas-Barberan 2014 urolithin metabotypes; Andreux 2019 Nature Metabolism urolithin-A human RCT

dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.ceiling: FDA 2023 qualified health claim requires ~200 mg cocoa flavanols/day for CV benefit; alkalization (Dutching) destroys flavanols; non-alkalized cacao retains them but content is brand- and batch-variable without COA
dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.source: FDA 2023 qualified health claim on cocoa flavanols; COSMOS trial (PMID 35381708)

dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.ceiling: EFSA 2016 TDI 7 mg/kg bw/day; easily exceeded at 1-2 tbsp/day mustard oil (30-54% erucic)
Meta-analytic HR 0.94 per 50g/day cooked, data range ~0-150g/day; linear extrapolation beyond 150g not supported. Typical Indian dal intake already in 80-150g range — near saturation.
dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.source: Darmadi-Blackberry 2004 (Asia Pac J Clin Nutr); Schwingshackl 2017 meta-analysis

dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.ceiling: IOM tolerable UL 4000 IU/day in adults; toxicity (hypercalcemia) at sustained >10,000 IU/day; Indian deficiency prevalence high (~70-90%) so baseline 2000 IU/day reasonable but dose should be titrated to 25(OH)D level
dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.source: IOM 2011 Dietary Reference Intakes for Calcium and Vitamin D; Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline 2011/2024

dose_ceilings_and_bioavailability.ceiling: 3-5 g/day maintenance is well-tolerated; loading (20 g/day x 5-7 days) causes GI upset in some; caution in severe renal impairment (eGFR <30) though creatine itself is not nephrotoxic in healthy subjects

bioavailability_conditioners.note: Requires piperine (20 mg per 2g curcumin for ~20x AUC) or lipid co-ingestion for meaningful absorption; aqueous curcumin powder alone is ~1% absorbed

Requires myrosinase enzyme; dried/heat-processed moringa powder has near-zero active myrosinase. Use fresh leaves OR co-ingest with a myrosinase source (crushed mustard seed, daikon radish, fresh rocket/arugula)
Same myrosinase rule — cooked broccoli loses enzyme; sprouts or raw florets + 30-min chop-rest maximize sulforaphane yield
Fat-soluble — absorb best with a meal containing fat; empty-stomach dosing markedly reduces uptake

bioavailability_conditioners.compound: iron from plant sources (amla, moringa, greens)
bioavailability_conditioners.note: Non-heme iron absorption enhanced by co-ingested vitamin C (amla pairing is functional); inhibited by tea/coffee tannins and calcium within 1 hour
Triglyceride and re-esterified TG forms have better absorption than ethyl-ester forms; take with fat-containing meal; oxidation state (peroxide value) matters — avoid rancid oils

Absorption depends heavily on gut microbiome; intact anthocyanins are <1% bioavailable but protocatechuic-acid and phenolic-acid metabolites (produced by gut bacteria) carry most biological activity. Freezing preserves; shelf oxidation degrades fresh berries.

Fresh amla ≠ murabba ≠ dried powder ≠ chyawanprash. Processed/candied amla loses 30-70% vitamin C; chyawanprash amla may retain <20%. Tannins in amla delay oxidation relative to synthetic ascorbate — the matrix matters.

Alkalization (Dutching) destroys 60-90% of flavanols. 'Raw' Indian cacao usually means non-alkalized (may be lightly fermented/sun-dried) — plausibly flavanol-retaining but quantitation requires HPLC COA.

Extremely inefficient (~60:1 tryptophan:niacin equivalent); dietary dal tryptophan does NOT meaningfully raise NAD+; serotonin synthesis competes for tryptophan. Niacin / NR are much more efficient precursors.

Published 35-90 mg/100g figures are DRY weight. 50g fresh oyster mushroom ≈ 5g dry → 1.5-4 mg ergothioneine per serving. To hit the ~5 mg/day maintenance target, fresh-weight servings may need to be ~100-150g or supplemented.

seasonal_availability.frozen_backup: Frozen jamun pulp and freeze-dried jamun powder retain anthocyanins reasonably (~70-85% vs fresh); commercial availability limited — regional online sellers. Recommend purchasing in season and freezing at home.
seasonal_availability.source_pmid_or_guideline: IFCT 2017 NIN India; general tropical fruit seasonality references

seasonal_availability.frozen_backup: Frozen amla pieces retain >80% vitamin C; amla juice (bottled, no added sugar) acceptable; dried powder and murabba LOSE substantial vitamin C (see bioavailability section). Recommend freezing fresh in season.

seasonal_availability.months_available: September-February (peak Oct-Dec); Maharashtra year-round cultivation gives extended but variable availability
seasonal_availability.frozen_backup: Frozen arils widely available at quick-commerce (₹300-400/500g); retain ellagitannins well. Cold-pressed NFC juice (not from concentrate) is acceptable off-season.

seasonal_availability.months_available: October harvest; shelled nuts available year-round in cold storage but freshest Oct-Feb
seasonal_availability.frozen_backup: Vacuum-sealed refrigerated/frozen storage preserves ALA (omega-3 is heat/oxidation-sensitive). Avoid rancid stock — smell-check.

seasonal_availability.months_available: March-October in most of peninsular India; can be year-round in South India; dried powder year-round
seasonal_availability.frozen_backup: Blanched + frozen moringa leaves retain most glucomoringin and partial myrosinase; drying destroys myrosinase. Home-grown preferred given heavy-metal variability in commercial supply.
Home-sprouting is trivial and cheaper than store-bought; broccoli seeds available year-round from Indian seed retailers. No frozen/dried substitute matches fresh sprout sulforaphane yield.

seasonal_availability.months_available: Imported blueberry available year-round via quick-commerce; Indian strawberry (Mahabaleshwar/Nainital) peak Nov-March
seasonal_availability.frozen_backup: Frozen blueberry (₹169-250/150g) is nutritionally comparable or superior for anthocyanins — freezing preserves, shelf oxidation degrades fresh. Frozen is a valid middle-ground option that price-file spot-check confirmed.
seasonal_availability.source_pmid_or_guideline: USDA FoodData Central; anthocyanin stability in frozen vs fresh literature

seasonal_availability.months_available: Year-round at urban modern trade; oyster and button most widely available; shiitake fresh limited to Tier-1 cities, dried shiitake year-round
seasonal_availability.frozen_backup: Dried shiitake (reconstituted) retains ergothioneine well; sun-dried shiitake actually has elevated vitamin D2 (UV exposure). Use dried for steady supply.

price_and_market_safety_notes.note: Quick-commerce URL link-rot: BigBasket, Zepto, Blinkit SKU URLs expire. Every verified price claim should also record an archive.org Wayback timestamp and a vendor category URL as fallback. Monthly re-verification required before launch.
Promotional pricing contamination: nuturge macadamia ₹780 low-end was a discount floor, not steady-state. Use non-discounted anchors (₹1,239-1,500) as consumer-facing baseline.

Dose-price mapping for branded ashwagandha: ₹350/month is consistent with generic ashwagandha powder, not patent-protected KSM-66 at clinical dose (300-600 mg/day). Branded KSM-66 (Rasayanam, MuscleBlaze) is ₹550-700/month. Do not quote low prices without naming SKU + mg/dose + capsule count.

cohort_generalizability_flags.cohort: Laukkanen 2015 Finnish sauna — 2,315 men, traditional dry-humid Finnish sauna, lifelong adaptation
cohort_generalizability_flags.generalizability_gap: Indian women, infrared-sauna users, hot-climate baseline heat stress, no cultural adaptation

cohort_generalizability_flags.cohort: Singapore MCI ergothioneine cohort — ethnic Chinese
cohort_generalizability_flags.generalizability_gap: Direct transfer to Indian populations unvalidated; OCTN1 genotype frequencies differ

cohort_generalizability_flags.cohort: Adventist Health Study-2 nuts/legumes — self-selected US vegetarian, low-alcohol, low-smoking, suburban
cohort_generalizability_flags.generalizability_gap: Healthy-user confounding; US caloric/macronutrient baseline; Indian dal intake already near-saturation

cohort_generalizability_flags.cohort: SPRINT 2015 intensive BP — excluded diabetes, prior stroke, frail >80
cohort_generalizability_flags.generalizability_gap: Indian hypertensive population has high diabetes comorbidity; generalizability poor

cohort_generalizability_flags.cohort: PURE exercise — HR 0.65 at high-PA vs low-PA extremes
cohort_generalizability_flags.generalizability_gap: At guideline-minimum dose (150 min/week), mortality reduction is ~20-25%, not 35%. Dose-response plateau at 3-5x guidelines. Indian non-recreational (occupational/transport) PA counts equally.

regulatory_flags_india.regulation: FSSAI Nutraceutical Regulations 2022 (Schedule VII permitted ingredients)
regulatory_flags_india.action: Do not recommend purchase pathways for non-listed ingredients
Only recommend CDSCO-registered devices; record registration numbers

regulatory_flags_india.regulation: ICMR-DBT National Guidelines for Stem Cell Research 2017 / DCGI
regulatory_flags_india.action: Mark as not legal / not approved in India — context only
Use general-wellness language; avoid disease-treatment/prevention framing in consumer copy

affected_items: all nutrient-function and disease-risk reduction claims
regulatory_flags_india.action: Substantiation dossier required before commercial promotion of specific health claims
Require COA-backed brands; do not recommend unsourced commercial powders
Disclose paid partnerships on every affiliate link

gaps_not_sourced_from_review_files.gap: Indian-specific APOE ε4 allele frequencies by region — mentioned but not quantified in review files
MTHFR C677T/A1298C Indian-population prevalence — not in review files
